Title: Trehalose-6-phosphate phosphatase E modulates ABA-controlled root growth and stomatal movement in Arabidopsis

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point under ABA-responsive conditions; strongest ABA-induced TPP family member in this study
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress-protective compatible sugar; enhances ABA responses associated with root growth inhibition and stomatal closure via ROS accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seedlings; roots/root tips; guard cells/stomatal tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced by ABA; reaches maximal induction around 3 h after ABA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ABA strongly induces expression in seedlings; promoter GUS activity increases after ABA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA treatment (30-50 µM); exogenous trehalose; ROS-related treatments GSH and DPI
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S (constitutive overexpression); native TPPE promoter (inducible by ABA in reporter assays)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutants; CRISPR/Cas9 mutants; overexpression lines; LC-MS trehalose quantification; in vitro enzyme assay with T6P/G6P/S6P; ABA root elongation assay; stomatal aperture assay; water-loss assay; ROS staining
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA-induced trehalose accumulation, root growth inhibition, stomatal closure, and ROS accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPPE is the most strongly ABA-induced member of the Arabidopsis TPP family. 2. TPPE preferentially uses T6P as substrate and increases trehalose accumulation in vivo. 3. Loss of TPPE reduces trehalose content and weakens ABA inhibition of root elongation and ABA-induced stomatal closure. 4. TPPE overexpression increases trehalose content, ABA sensitivity of root growth, and ROS accumulation. 5. ABA-induced trehalose accumulation is largely abolished in tppe mutants, placing TPPE as a key mediator downstream of ABA signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TPPE complements yeast tps2 mutant function. Overexpression enhanced ROS and root ABA sensitivity, but did not significantly alter detached-leaf water loss relative to WT, indicating tissue-specific or network-buffered effects on stomata.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced TPPE expression increases ABA sensitivity and root growth inhibition; possible growth restraint under stress-signaling conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering stress-responsive trehalose accumulation and ABA responsiveness, but constitutive enhancement may penalize root growth; inducible or stress-tuned expression would be preferable

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bZIP transcription factor; ABA-responsive element binding factor
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates TPPE transcription by binding ABRE-containing promoter region
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator linking ABA signaling to TPPE induction and trehalose accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress-protective compatible sugar contributing to ABA-regulated root growth inhibition and stomatal closure through ROS accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Functions during vegetative ABA responses; expression details not fully reported here
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed in seedlings and protoplasts under ABA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S (constitutive overexpression); TPPE native promoter and ABRE multimer reporters
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; transient luciferase assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis protoplast dual-luciferase assay; T-DNA mutant analysis; genetic epistasis with 35S:ABF2/tppe-1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA-induced TPPE expression and trehalose accumulation, thereby enhancing ABA inhibition of root elongation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABF2 directly binds the ABRE-containing TPPE promoter in vitro and in vivo. 2. ABF2 activates TPPE transcription, and this activation is enhanced by ABA. 3. abf2 mutants show reduced TPPE expression and fail to accumulate trehalose normally after ABA treatment. 4. tppe mutation suppresses the ABA-hypersensitive root phenotype of 35S:ABF2, indicating TPPE acts downstream of ABF2.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper reports no evidence that ABF3 or ABF4 directly bind the TPPE promoter under the tested conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced ABF2 activity may increase ABA sensitivity and growth inhibition under stress-signaling conditions
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream regulator for tuning ABA-responsive trehalose biosynthesis, but likely requires conditional deployment to avoid excess growth suppression
